Core Gameplay Loop.observations: The tap-to-count mechanic is simple and large-target friendly — the left/right halves of the screen are generous tap zones, which helps with motor imprecision. However, there is no haptic or audio feedback to confirm a tap registered, which is a significant gap for users who rely on non-visual confirmation. The Graveyard buttons are clearly labeled with text, which is good, but the scoring reset mechanic (zeroing the opponent's score) is not explained anywhere, making it confusing without a tutorial.

UX / Usability.observations: The pause button ('II') is a small icon-only control at top center — no label, no enlarged hit target, no accessible name for screen readers. The score labels 'L:' and 'R:' are positionally differentiated but not color-differentiated or otherwise distinguished beyond abbreviations, which is ambiguous. No font size toggle, no high-contrast mode, and no screen reader metadata (accessible labels, focus order) are present. The main menu buttons appear functional but there is no indication of button state or focus highlighting for motor/visual users who navigate slowly.

Visual Quality.observations: The 72pt score font is a strong positive — it is large enough for most low-vision users to read at a glance. The pastoral 3D scene is visually coherent. However, the white cow with black spots against a varied outdoor background may blend in under certain lighting conditions, and there is no option to increase contrast or enable a colorblind-friendly palette. The gray gravestone against the road/hills environment could be difficult to distinguish for users with contrast sensitivity issues.

Game Feel.observations: No haptic feedback means users with visual impairments get zero tactile confirmation that a tap registered. No audio cues (sound effects, tones, or spoken score readout) means users who rely on auditory feedback are completely unserved. There is no animation or visual flash on successful tap to reinforce the action for users who may miss subtle UI changes. The overall feel is silent and unresponsive to accessibility needs.

Visual Correctness.observations: Scores appear to be correctly displayed in their respective corners and the labels are consistent. The 3D scene renders the expected objects (cow, gravestone, road, grass, sky). No obvious visual glitches reported. However, there is no alt-text equivalent for scene elements, and the score display does not announce updates in any way that a screen reader could intercept.

Performance Perception.observations: No performance issues are noted in the current build description. The Mobile renderer and Godot 4.6.1 foundation appears stable. From an accessibility standpoint, smooth performance matters for users with vestibular sensitivities — no reported motion sickness triggers or jarring frame drops. No reduced-motion setting exists, but the game's camera appears static, which is passively helpful.

top_strengths: Large tap zones (left/right screen halves) are naturally motor-accessible and forgiving of imprecise touches
72pt score font is large enough for most low-vision users without requiring any adjustment
Graveyard action buttons use full text labels rather than icons only, reducing ambiguity
top_issues: No haptic or audio feedback on any action — users with visual impairments have no non-visual way to confirm taps or score changes
Pause button is a small unlabeled icon with no accessible name or enlarged hit target, creating a barrier for both motor and screen reader users
No accessibility settings at all — no font size toggle, no high-contrast mode, no colorblind palette, no screen reader support — making the game inaccessible by default for a wide range of users
verdict: The game has a naturally accessible tap mechanic and large score text, but the complete absence of audio feedback, haptic response, accessible labels, and any accessibility settings makes it very difficult to play comfortably with visual or motor accessibility needs.
